behaviors.description: Extract all direct and transitive dependencies from manifests and include them in the vulnerability-check input set.
behaviors.justification: Failure is at module handoff (manifest parser -> dependency graph/resolution -> scanner input). A unit test of only parsing or only graph traversal can miss dropped transitive nodes. No behavior text requires real OS/network/subprocess, and no AI output is involved, so System/Agentic/Workflow are disqualified.
behaviors.test_description: Use fixture manifests/lockfiles and run the real in-process extraction pipeline end-to-end; assert the emitted dependency set includes every expected direct and transitive package/version and excludes none.

behaviors.description: Ensure vulnerability matching is version-range exact (package + version constraints), not package-name-only.
behaviors.justification: This checks query-building plus response-matching logic across modules (request construction, matcher/filter, result mapper). A fake API response can still expose name-only matching bugs if version constraints are varied. Higher levels were rejected because behavior text does not require real network quirks or external API runtime behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Wire scanner service to a deterministic stub vulnerability API and run queries for same package across multiple manifest version ranges; assert only CVEs matching exact requested ranges are returned.

behaviors.description: Sort vulnerabilities by severity order (Critical > High > Medium > Low), then by CVSS within equal severity.
behaviors.justification: Behavior is deterministic ordering logic within one function/object boundary. No module handoff, real infra, or AI dependency is needed to reproduce failures.
behaviors.test_description: Call the sort function with mixed severities and CVSS ties; assert strict global ordering and stable tie-break behavior for equal severity.

behaviors.description: Modify manifest so only the affected dependency version changes; all other manifest entries remain unchanged.
behaviors.justification: Real failure usually appears across parse -> mutate -> serialize stages (field preservation, unintended rewrites). Unit-testing only mutation logic can miss serializer side effects. System rejected because local in-process file/module wiring is sufficient; no external OS/network/subprocess behavior is required.
behaviors.test_description: Run manifest update pipeline on representative files; compare before/after structure and content to confirm exactly one dependency version changed and every other entry/value remained identical.

behaviors.description: Create the auto-generated pull request on the real repository with a correct single-dependency diff.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires real repository PR creation, i.e., real HTTP/API + git hosting integration. Mocks can hide auth/permission/branch/diff-upload issues. Agentic/Workflow rejected because no LLM decision-making is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute scanner against a real test repository/project and verify an actual PR is opened remotely; assert the PR diff contains only the intended dependency bump and no unrelated file changes.

behaviors.description: Identify a major version bump as breaking via semver comparison.
behaviors.justification: Pure semver rule evaluation (version A -> version B) is deterministic function logic. No multi-module boundary or external infra is required.
behaviors.test_description: Test semver comparison function with major/minor/patch transitions and pre-release edge cases; assert only major increments are flagged as breaking.

behaviors.description: When breaking change is detected, skip PR creation and surface a review flag in scanner output.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is orchestration/state handoff: semver decision -> PR workflow gate -> reporting output. A unit test of one component can miss incorrect coordination. System rejected because real repo/API is not required to verify skip behavior; stub PR client is sufficient. No AI dependency.
behaviors.test_description: Run scanner flow with a detected breaking upgrade using a stub PR client; assert no PR-create call occurs and scanner output includes the expected review-required flag/context.
